core_payload.content:
# CapsuleOS: The Sovereign Operating System for Knowledge

## Dream Direction
In the Dream branch, CapsuleOS remains future doctrine for how the sovereign memory system should mature beyond the already-live Real contract. Dream does not restate current validator-owned truth. It carries the next operating horizon.

## Future Operating Delta
- richer branch-aware control surfaces for inspection, retrieval, validation, and promotion review
- clearer decomposition of CapsuleOS into smaller atomic laws when the current hub becomes too dense
- stronger lifecycle observability so ingest, Dream landing, review, promotion, quarantine, and archive stay inspectable at scale
- better agent-facing stewardship interfaces without weakening validator sovereignty
- a calmer second-brain operating model where memory routing, branch discipline, and upkeep feel explicit rather than implied

## Why Dream Stays Separate
Real already owns the live CapsuleOS contract. Dream is kept separate so future operating-system improvements can be explored without mutating the current admission boundary, capsule law, or validator-governed trust model before they are ready.

## Future Pressure to Watch
- split large doctrinal surfaces when CapsuleOS law becomes too broad for one hub
- improve branch-facing review ergonomics without collapsing Dream into runtime residue
- keep future agent control surfaces compatible with validator sovereignty and promotion discipline

neuro_concentrate.summary: CapsuleOS Foundation in the Dream branch preserves only the future operating delta beyond the already-live Real contract. Dream focuses on richer branch-aware control surfaces, clearer doctrinal decomposition, stronger lifecycle observability, and better stewardship interfaces for a sovereign second-brain system. It does not redefine current validator-owned trust law. Instead, it protects a space where CapsuleOS can mature without confusing future operating-system improvements with the present admission boundary that already governs capsules in the repository.
